title: EvaluatorSimulation
description: Adversarial evaluator simulation report. Extends review_report.json with per-criterion predicted scores, hard-rejection checks, and funding probability assessment. Produced by adversarial_evaluator_simulator.

simulated_evaluator_profiles.description: Descriptions of the evaluator personas used in the simulation

hard_rejection_checks.description: Hard-rejection and minimum-threshold checks run before any scoring

met.description: Whether the check is currently met based on draft content

hard_rejection_risk.description: true if failing this check would trigger automatic rejection

evidence.description: What in the draft (or absence thereof) leads to this assessment

action_required.description: What must be done to satisfy this check. Empty string if already met.

criterion_scores.description: Predicted score per criterion/sub-criterion from evaluation_matrix.json

criterion_id.description: Matches criterion_id in evaluation_matrix.json (e.g. C1, C2.1, C3.2)

weight.description: Weight multiplier from evaluation_matrix.json (1 if unweighted)

predicted_score_central.description: Central estimate — use this for total score calculation

score_rationale.description: Why this score is predicted — reference specific strengths and weaknesses in the draft

weakest_argument.description: The single argument in this section that Evaluator A/B/C is most likely to downgrade

score_ceiling.description: Maximum achievable score given current evidence and project status — not the theoretical max

improvement_actions.description: Specific actions to raise the predicted score for this criterion

hard_rejection_risks_detected.description: List of hard-rejection checks that are not met — these must be resolved before submission

funding_probability.description: high = likely funded; medium = competitive but not assured; low = unlikely without major revision; at_risk = hard-rejection risk present

percentile_estimate.description: Estimated percentile position in the field of competing proposals (e.g. 'top 20-30%')

single_highest_impact_action.description: The one action that would most improve the predicted total score

improvement_actions_ranked.description: Top 5 improvement actions ranked by estimated score gain
